{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan02s3.jpg|300px]] | name = Yasaka Shrine | location = [[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Kokurakita Ward]], {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|12=draft}} 2-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|07.68|N|130|52|25.39|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yasaka Shrine}} | Deities = North Hall:
{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐能袁命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之菩卑命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|天津日子根命|1=Amatsuhikone|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|活津日子根命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|熊野久須比命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市寸島比売命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理比売命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津比売命|12=draft}}
South Hall:
{{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|ja|大国主|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|12=draft}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|12=draft}} in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|12=draft}}, [[Kokurakita Ward]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous ranking under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|12=draft}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|12=draft}}'' featuring actors like {{ill|Bando Tsumasaburō|ja|阪東妻三郎|12=draft}} (1943 version) and {{ill|Mifune Toshirō|ja|三船敏郎|12=draft}} (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|12=draft}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft}} movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|12=draft}}. == Timeline == * {{ill|Jōgan (era)|ja|貞観|12=draft}} 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|12=draft}} from Yasaka in [[Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|12=draft}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|12=draft}}. * [[Keichō]] 7 (1602) – {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|12=draft}} begins constructing [[Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|12=draft}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|12=draft}} in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|12=draft}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|12=draft}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[Kokura Castle]] * {{ill|Riverwalk Kitakyushu|ja|リバーウォーク北九州|12=draft}} * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yasaka Shrine Kitakyushu}} {{citation needed|Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)}} {{citation needed|Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Kokurakita Ward History]] [[Category:Kokurakita Ward Architecture]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%8C%97%E4%B9%9D%E5%B7%9E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[en:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%8C%97%E4%B9%9D%E5%B7%9E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:asaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)}}

{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐能袁命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之菩卑命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|天津日子根命|1=Amatsuhikone|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|活津日子根命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|熊野久須比命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市寸島比売命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理比売命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津比売命|12=draft|12=draft}}
South Hall:
{{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|12=draft|12=draft}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|12=draft|12=draft}} in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Kokurakita Ward]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|12=draft|12=draft}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|12=draft|12=draft}}'' featuring actors like {{ill|Bando Tsumasaburō|ja|阪東妻三郎|12=draft|12=draft}} (1943 version) and {{ill|Mifune Toshirō|ja|三船敏郎|12=draft|12=draft}} (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|12=draft|12=draft}} movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Timeline == * {{ill|Jōgan (era)|ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|12=draft|12=draft}} from Yasaka in [[Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|12=draft|12=draft}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|12=draft|12=draft}}. * [[Keichō]] 7 (1602) – {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|12=draft|12=draft}} begins constructing [[Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|12=draft|12=draft}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|12=draft|12=draft}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[Kokura Castle]] * {{ill|Riverwalk Kitakyushu|ja|リバーウォーク北九州|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yasaka Shrine Kitakyushu}} {{citation needed|Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)}} {{citation needed|Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| (Kitakyushu)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[en:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE revision.comment: 48 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:八坂神社 (北九州市) up to rev 103722451
revision.text:
{{神社 |画像= [[ファイル:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan02s3.jpg|300px]] |名称= 八坂神社 |所在地= [[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[小倉北区]][[城内 (北九州市)|城内]]2-2 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|53|07.68|N|130|52|25.39|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=八坂神社}} |祭神= 北殿
[[櫛名田比売命]]、[[スサノオ|須佐能袁命]]、[[アメノホヒ|天之菩卑命]]、[[天之忍穂耳命]]、[[天津日子根命]]、[[活津日子根命]]、[[クマノクスビ|熊野久須比命]]、[[市寸島比売命]]、[[タキリビメ|多紀理比売命]]、[[タギツヒメ|多岐津比売命]]
南殿
[[大国主|大名牟遅命]]、[[スクナビコナ|少比古那命]]、[[須佐之男命]]、[[櫛名田比売命]] |社格= [[県社]]（[[明治]]6年5月15日） |創建= [[細川忠興]][[元和]]3年（1617年）2月 |本殿= 流造 |例祭= 祇園祭（[[小倉祇園太鼓]]）7月の第3土曜日・日曜日 |神事= }} [[ファイル:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|拝殿]] [[ファイル:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|本殿]] [[ファイル:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|東門]] '''小倉祇園八坂神社'''（こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[小倉北区]][[城内 (北九州市)|城内]]（[[小倉城]]内）にある[[神社]]。祭神はテンプレートを参照。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。[[神社本庁]]に属さない[[単立|単立神社]]である。 == 概要 == 古くは「こくらのぎおんさん」と呼ばれていた。[[阪東妻三郎]]（1943年版）や[[三船敏郎]]（1958年版）らが主演した映画『[[無法松の一生]]』に登場する[[小倉祇園太鼓]]はこの神社の[[例大祭]]である。祭は[[1618年]]（[[元和 (日本)|元和]]4年）にはじまり、約390年の歴史を有する。現在は、毎年7月の第3土曜日・日曜日に開催されている。 もともと小倉藩内にあった[[祇園社]]を移した経緯から、当初は単に「祇園社」と呼ばれていたが、[[1868年]]（[[慶応]]4年）の[[神仏分離令]]（[[廃仏毀釈]]運動）を受け、[[1871年]]（明治4年）に「八坂神社」へと改められた{{Cite journal|title=小倉における祭礼形態と都市構造の近代化に関する研究-小倉祇園祭巡行路からみて-|author=山﨑信弥|url=https://www.arch.kyushu-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/06.pdf|date=2021-03|journal=日本建築学会九州支部研究報告集|volume=50|pages=501-504|publsher=日本建築学会九州支部|accessdate=2025-03-13}}。 == 年表 == * [[870年]]（[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]12年）4月 - 疫病が流行したため、[[山城国]]八坂郷の祇園社を長尾（[[小倉南区]]長尾周辺）に勧請し、[[企救郡]]の総鎮守とする。 * [[1602年]]（[[慶長]]7年） - [[細川忠興]]が[[小倉城]]築城開始。 * [[1617年]]（[[元和 (日本)|元和]]3年） - [[小倉藩]]の[[総鎮守]]として城北の清地鋳物師町に南殿北殿のある祇園社を建てる。これが八坂神社の直接の前身である。南殿には長尾の祇園社を、北殿には三本松（小倉北区古船場町付近）の祇園社を移した{{R|yamazaki2021}}。なお、長尾の祇園社は存続し、三本松の祇園社には高倉稲荷神社が鎮座した{{R|yamazaki2021}}。 * [[1618年]]（[[元和 (日本)|元和]]4年） - 京都出身の細川忠興により、京都の[[祇園祭]]を取り入れ、小倉祇園がはじめられた。元々は京都祇園祭が原型の、飾り付けをした[[山車]]が用いられていた。 * [[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）[[5月15日]]、県社に列せられる。 * [[1934年]]（昭和9年） - 鉄道開通や工場進出により鋳物師町の立地が煙害を受けるようになり、小倉城内に移転{{R|yamazaki2021}}。跡地は国鉄の社宅となる{{R|yamazaki2021}}。 * [[1943年]]（昭和18年）[[10月28日]] - [[岩下俊作]]原作の小説『[[富島松五郎伝]]』が、映画『[[無法松の一生]]』として公開され、[[小倉祇園太鼓]]が全国的に知られるようになった。 * [[1958年]]（昭和33年）[[4月3日]] - 小倉祇園太鼓が福岡県指定[[無形民俗文化財]]に指定。 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年） - 小倉祇園太鼓が現行の開催日程に変更された。 * [[2000年]]（平成12年） - 三本松（古船場町）の祇園社跡地にあった高倉稲荷神社を合祀{{R|yamazaki2021}}。跡地にはマンション「ニューサンリバー12番館」が建設された。 == アクセス == * [[西小倉駅]]より徒歩4分(350m) * [[北九州高速1号線]][[大手町出入口]]より1.5㎞、[[勝山出入口]]より1.1㎞ == 周辺施設 == *[[小倉城]] *[[リバーウォーク北九州]] *[[北九州市役所]] *[[勝山公園 (北九州市)|勝山公園]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} *[http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ 八坂神社] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やさかしんしや きたきゆうしゆうし}} [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:小倉北区の歴史]] [[Category:小倉北区の建築物]] [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]]

{{神社 |画像= [[ファイル:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan02s3.jpg|300px]] |名称= 八坂神社 |所在地= [[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[小倉北区]][[城内 (北九州市)|城内]]2-2 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|53|07.68|N|130|52|25.39|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=八坂神社}} |祭神= 北殿
[[櫛名田比売命]]、[[スサノオ|須佐能袁命]]、[[アメノホヒ|天之菩卑命]]、[[天之忍穂耳命]]、[[天津日子根命]]、[[活津日子根命]]、[[クマノクスビ|熊野久須比命]]、[[市寸島比売命]]、[[タキリビメ|多紀理比売命]]、[[タギツヒメ|多岐津比売命]]
南殿
[[大国主|大名牟遅命]]、[[スクナビコナ|少比古那命]]、[[須佐之男命]]、[[櫛名田比売命]] |社格= [[県社]]（[[明治]]6年5月15日） |創建= [[細川忠興]][[元和]]3年（1617年）2月 |本殿= 流造 |例祭= 祇園祭（[[小倉祇園太鼓]]）7月の第3土曜日・日曜日 |神事= }} [[ファイル:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|拝殿]] [[ファイル:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|本殿]] [[ファイル:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|東門]] '''小倉祇園八坂神社'''（こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[小倉北区]][[城内 (北九州市)|城内]]（[[小倉城]]内）にある[[神社]]。祭神はテンプレートを参照。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。[[神社本庁]]に属さない[[単立|単立神社]]である。 == 概要 == 古くは「こくらのぎおんさん」と呼ばれていた。[[阪東妻三郎]]（1943年版）や[[三船敏郎]]（1958年版）らが主演した映画『[[無法松の一生]]』に登場する[[小倉祇園太鼓]]はこの神社の[[例大祭]]である。祭は[[1618年]]（[[元和 (日本)|元和]]4年）にはじまり、約390年の歴史を有する。現在は、毎年7月の第3土曜日・日曜日に開催されている。 もともと小倉藩内にあった[[祇園社]]を移した経緯から、当初は単に「祇園社」と呼ばれていたが、[[1868年]]（[[慶応]]4年）の[[神仏分離令]]（[[廃仏毀釈]]運動）を受け、[[1871年]]（明治4年）に「八坂神社」へと改められた{{Cite journal|title=小倉における祭礼形態と都市構造の近代化に関する研究-小倉祇園祭巡行路からみて-|author=山﨑信弥|url=https://www.arch.kyushu-u.ac.jp/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/06.pdf|date=2021-03|journal=日本建築学会九州支部研究報告集|volume=50|pages=501-504|publsher=日本建築学会九州支部|accessdate=2025-03-13}}。 == 年表 == * [[870年]]（[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]12年）4月 - 疫病が流行したため、[[山城国]]八坂郷の祇園社を長尾（[[小倉南区]]長尾周辺）に勧請し、[[企救郡]]の総鎮守とする。 * [[1602年]]（[[慶長]]7年） - [[細川忠興]]が[[小倉城]]築城開始。 * [[1617年]]（[[元和 (日本)|元和]]3年） - [[小倉藩]]の[[総鎮守]]として城北の清地鋳物師町に南殿北殿のある祇園社を建てる。これが八坂神社の直接の前身である。南殿には長尾の祇園社を、北殿には三本松（小倉北区古船場町付近）の祇園社を移した{{R|yamazaki2021}}。なお、長尾の祇園社は存続し、三本松の祇園社には高倉稲荷神社が鎮座した{{R|yamazaki2021}}。 * [[1618年]]（[[元和 (日本)|元和]]4年） - 京都出身の細川忠興により、京都の[[祇園祭]]を取り入れ、小倉祇園がはじめられた。元々は京都祇園祭が原型の、飾り付けをした[[山車]]が用いられていた。 * [[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）[[5月15日]]、県社に列せられる。 * [[1934年]]（昭和9年） - 鉄道開通や工場進出により鋳物師町の立地が煙害を受けるようになり、小倉城内に移転{{R|yamazaki2021}}。跡地は国鉄の社宅となる{{R|yamazaki2021}}。 * [[1943年]]（昭和18年）[[10月28日]] - [[岩下俊作]]原作の小説『[[富島松五郎伝]]』が、映画『[[無法松の一生]]』として公開され、[[小倉祇園太鼓]]が全国的に知られるようになった。 * [[1958年]]（昭和33年）[[4月3日]] - 小倉祇園太鼓が福岡県指定[[無形民俗文化財]]に指定。 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年） - 小倉祇園太鼓が現行の開催日程に変更された。 * [[2000年]]（平成12年） - 三本松（古船場町）の祇園社跡地にあった高倉稲荷神社を合祀{{R|yamazaki2021}}。跡地にはマンション「ニューサンリバー12番館」が建設された。 == アクセス == * [[西小倉駅]]より徒歩4分(350m) * [[北九州高速1号線]][[大手町出入口]]より1.5㎞、[[勝山出入口]]より1.1㎞ == 周辺施設 == *[[小倉城]] *[[リバーウォーク北九州]] *[[北九州市役所]] *[[勝山公園 (北九州市)|勝山公園]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} *[http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ 八坂神社] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やさかしんしや きたきゆうしゆうし}} [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:小倉北区の歴史]] [[Category:小倉北区の建築物]] [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan02s3.jpg|300px]] | name = Yasaka Shrine | location = [[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Kokurakita Ward]], {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|07.68|N|130|52|25.39|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yasaka Shrine}} | Deities = North Hall:
{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐能袁命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之菩卑命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|天津日子根命|1=Amatsuhikone|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|熊野久須比命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市寸島比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
South Hall:
{{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Kokurakita Ward]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' featuring actors like {{ill|Bando Tsumasaburō|ja|阪東妻三郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1943 version) and {{ill|Mifune Toshirō|ja|三船敏郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Timeline == * {{ill|Jōgan (era)|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from Yasaka in [[Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * [[Keichō]] 7 (1602) – {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|qq=draft|qq=draft}} begins constructing [[Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[Kokura Castle]] * {{ill|Riverwalk Kitakyushu|ja|リバーウォーク北九州|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐能袁命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之菩卑命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|天津日子根命|1=Amatsuhikone|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|熊野久須比命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市寸島比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
South Hall:
{{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Kokurakita Ward]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' featuring actors like {{ill|Bando Tsumasaburō|ja|阪東妻三郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1943 version) and {{ill|Mifune Toshirō|ja|三船敏郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Timeline == * {{ill|Jōgan (era)|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from Yasaka in [[Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * [[Keichō]] 7 (1602) – {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|qq=draft|qq=draft}} begins constructing [[Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[Kokura Castle]] * {{ill|Riverwalk Kitakyushu|ja|リバーウォーク北九州|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} South Hall:
{{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Kokurakita Ward]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' featuring actors like {{ill|Bando Tsumasaburō|ja|阪東妻三郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1943 version) and {{ill|Mifune Toshirō|ja|三船敏郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Timeline == * {{ill|Jōgan (era)|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from Yasaka in [[Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * [[Keichō]] 7 (1602) – {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|qq=draft|qq=draft}} begins constructing [[Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[Kokura Castle]] * {{ill|Riverwalk Kitakyushu|ja|リバーウォーク北九州|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (北九州市)|version=103722451|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Kokurakita Ward History]] [[Category:Kokurakita Ward Architecture]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%8C%97%E4%B9%9D%E5%B7%9E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[en:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%8C%97%E4%B9%9D%E5%B7%9E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:asaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]]

{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐能袁命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之菩卑命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|天津日子根命|1=Amatsuhikone|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|熊野久須比命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市寸島比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
South Hall:
{{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Kokurakita Ward]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' featuring actors like {{ill|Bando Tsumasaburō|ja|阪東妻三郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1943 version) and {{ill|Mifune Toshirō|ja|三船敏郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Timeline == * {{ill|Jōgan (era)|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from Yasaka in [[Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * [[Keichō]] 7 (1602) – {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|qq=draft|qq=draft}} begins constructing [[Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[Kokura Castle]] * {{ill|Riverwalk Kitakyushu|ja|リバーウォーク北九州|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Kokurakita Ward History]] [[Category:Kokurakita Ward Architecture]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%8C%97%E4%B9%9D%E5%B7%9E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] }} [[sn:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[en:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%8C%97%E4%B9%9D%E5%B7%9E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:asaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan02s3.jpg|300px]] | name = Yasaka Shrine | location = [[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Kokurakita Ward]], {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|qq=}} 2-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|07.68|N|130|52|25.39|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yasaka Shrine}} | Deities = North Hall:
{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐能袁命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之菩卑命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|天津日子根命|1=Amatsuhikone|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|熊野久須比命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市寸島比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
South Hall:
{{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Tadaoki|qq=}} in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|qq=}}, [[Kokurakita Ward]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Taiko|qq=}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Life of Matsu the Untamed|qq=}}'' featuring actors like {{ill|Bando Tsumasaburō|ja|阪東妻三郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bando Tsumasaburō|qq=}} (1943 version) and {{ill|Mifune Toshirō|ja|三船敏郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifune Toshirō|qq=}} (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|qq=}} movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}}. == Timeline == * {{ill|Jōgan (era)|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan (era)|qq=}} 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=}} from Yasaka in [[Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao, Kokuraminami|qq=}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiku District|qq=}}. * [[Keichō]] 7 (1602) – {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Tadaoki|qq=}} begins constructing [[Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanga Market|qq=}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}} in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtemachi Interchange|qq=}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Interchange|qq=}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[Kokura Castle]] * {{ill|Riverwalk Kitakyushu|ja|リバーウォーク北九州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Riverwalk Kitakyushu|qq=}} * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (北九州市)|version=103722451|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Kokurakita Ward History]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%8C%97%E4%B9%9D%E5%B7%9E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] }} [[sn:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[en:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%8C%97%E4%B9%9D%E5%B7%9E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:asaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]]

{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐能袁命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之菩卑命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|天津日子根命|1=Amatsuhikone|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|熊野久須比命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市寸島比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}

{{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Tadaoki|qq=}} in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|qq=}}, [[Kokurakita Ward]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Taiko|qq=}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Life of Matsu the Untamed|qq=}}'' featuring actors like {{ill|Bando Tsumasaburō|ja|阪東妻三郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bando Tsumasaburō|qq=}} (1943 version) and {{ill|Mifune Toshirō|ja|三船敏郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifune Toshirō|qq=}} (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|qq=}} movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}}. == Timeline == * {{ill|Jōgan (era)|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan (era)|qq=}} 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=}} from Yasaka in [[Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao, Kokuraminami|qq=}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiku District|qq=}}. * [[Keichō]] 7 (1602) – {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Tadaoki|qq=}} begins constructing [[Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanga Market|qq=}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}} in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtemachi Interchange|qq=}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Interchange|qq=}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[Kokura Castle]] * {{ill|Riverwalk Kitakyushu|ja|リバーウォーク北九州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Riverwalk Kitakyushu|qq=}} * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (北九州市)|version=103722451|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%8C%97%E4%B9%9D%E5%B7%9E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] }} [[sn:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[en:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Taiko|qq=}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Life of Matsu the Untamed|qq=}}'' featuring actors like {{ill|Bando Tsumasaburō|ja|阪東妻三郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bando Tsumasaburō|qq=}} (1943 version) and {{ill|Mifune Toshirō|ja|三船敏郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifune Toshirō|qq=}} (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|qq=}} movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}}. == Timeline == * {{ill|Jōgan (era)|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan (era)|qq=}} 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=}} from Yasaka in [[Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao, Kokuraminami|qq=}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiku District|qq=}}. * [[Keichō]] 7 (1602) – {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Tadaoki|qq=}} begins constructing [[Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanga Market|qq=}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}} in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtemachi Interchange|qq=}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Interchange|qq=}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[Kokura Castle]] * {{ill|Riverwalk Kitakyushu|ja|リバーウォーク北九州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Riverwalk Kitakyushu|qq=}} * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (北九州市)|version=103722451|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan02s3.jpg|300px]] | name = Yasaka Shrine | location = [[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Kokurakita Ward]], {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|qq=}} 2-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|07.68|N|130|52|25.39|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yasaka Shrine}} | Deities = North Hall:
{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐能袁命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之菩卑命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|天津日子根命|1=Amatsuhikone|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|熊野久須比命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市寸島比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
South Hall:
[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|qq=}}, [[Kokurakita Ward]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Taiko|qq=}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Life of Matsu the Untamed|qq=}}'' featuring actors like [[:en:Tsumasaburō Bandō|Bando Tsumasaburō]] (1943 version) and [[:en:Toshiro Mifune|Mifune Toshirō]] (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Timeline == * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (era)]] 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=}} from Yasaka in [[Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao, Kokuraminami|qq=}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiku District|qq=}}. * [[Keichō]] 7 (1602) – [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] begins constructing [[Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanga Market|qq=}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtemachi Interchange|qq=}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Interchange|qq=}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[Kokura Castle]] * [[:en:Riverwalk Kitakyūshū|Riverwalk Kitakyushu]] * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (北九州市)|version=103722451|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%8C%97%E4%B9%9D%E5%B7%9E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[en:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%8C%97%E4%B9%9D%E5%B7%9E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:asaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[Category:八坂神社]] {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐能袁命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之菩卑命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|天津日子根命|1=Amatsuhikone|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|熊野久須比命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市寸島比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}

[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|qq=}}, [[Kokurakita Ward]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Taiko|qq=}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Life of Matsu the Untamed|qq=}}'' featuring actors like [[:en:Tsumasaburō Bandō|Bando Tsumasaburō]] (1943 version) and [[:en:Toshiro Mifune|Mifune Toshirō]] (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Timeline == * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (era)]] 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=}} from Yasaka in [[Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao, Kokuraminami|qq=}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiku District|qq=}}. * [[Keichō]] 7 (1602) – [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] begins constructing [[Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanga Market|qq=}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtemachi Interchange|qq=}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Interchange|qq=}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[Kokura Castle]] * [[:en:Riverwalk Kitakyūshū|Riverwalk Kitakyushu]] * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (北九州市)|version=103722451|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] (Kitakyushu)]] [[en:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%8C%97%E4%B9%9D%E5%B7%9E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:asaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[Category:八坂神社]] The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanga Market|qq=}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐能袁命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之菩卑命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|天津日子根命|1=Amatsuhikone|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|熊野久須比命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市寸島比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
South Hall:
[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|qq=}}, [[Kokurakita Ward]], [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Taiko|qq=}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Life of Matsu the Untamed|qq=}}'' featuring actors like [[:en:Tsumasaburō Bandō|Bando Tsumasaburō]] (1943 version) and [[:en:Toshiro Mifune|Mifune Toshirō]] (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Timeline == * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (era)]] 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=}} from Yasaka in [[Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao, Kokuraminami|qq=}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiku District|qq=}}. * [[Keichō]] 7 (1602) – [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] begins constructing [[Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanga Market|qq=}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtemachi Interchange|qq=}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Interchange|qq=}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[Kokura Castle]] * [[:en:Riverwalk Kitakyūshū|Riverwalk Kitakyushu]] * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (北九州市)|version=103722451|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%8C%97%E4%B9%9D%E5%B7%9E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[ja:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%8C%97%E4%B9%9D%E5%B7%9E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:asaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Kokurakita-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:小倉北区の歴史]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] {{Commons category|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} [[ja:八坂神社 (北九州市)]]
