South Hall:
[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|qq=|1=Jōnai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425982}}, [[:en:Kokurakita Ward|Kokurakita Ward]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Taiko|qq=|1=Kokura Gion Daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459006}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Life of Matsu the Untamed|qq=|1=Muhōmatsu no Isshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24890846}}'' featuring actors like [[:en:Tsumasaburō Bandō|Bando Tsumasaburō]] (1943 version) and [[:en:Toshiro Mifune|Mifune Toshirō]] (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Timeline == * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (era)]] 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}} from Yasaka in [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao, Kokuraminami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiku District|qq=|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}. * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 7 (1602) – [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] begins constructing [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanga Market|qq=|1=Tanga Market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510878}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtemachi Interchange|qq=|1=Ōtemachi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258134}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Interchange|qq=|1=Katsuyama Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258218}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] * [[:en:Riverwalk Kitakyūshū|Riverwalk Kitakyushu]] * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|qq=|1=Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400235}} == (北九州市)|version=103722451|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] (%E5%8C%97%E4%B9%9D%E5%B7%9E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:asaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Kokurakita-ku, Kitakyushu]] South Hall:
[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|qq=|1=Jōnai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425982}}, [[:en:Kokurakita Ward|Kokurakita Ward]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Taiko|qq=|1=Kokura Gion Daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459006}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Life of Matsu the Untamed|qq=|1=Muhōmatsu no Isshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24890846}}'' featuring actors like [[:en:Tsumasaburō Bandō|Bando Tsumasaburō]] (1943 version) and [[:en:Toshiro Mifune|Mifune Toshirō]] (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Timeline == * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (era)]] 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}} from Yasaka in [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao, Kokuraminami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiku District|qq=|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}. * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 7 (1602) – [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] begins constructing [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanga Market|qq=|1=Tanga Market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510878}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtemachi Interchange|qq=|1=Ōtemachi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258134}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Interchange|qq=|1=Katsuyama Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258218}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] * [[:en:Riverwalk Kitakyūshū|Riverwalk Kitakyushu]] * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|qq=|1=Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400235}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 South Hall:
[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|qq=|1=Jōnai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425982}}, [[:en:Kokurakita Ward|Kokurakita Ward]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Taiko|qq=|1=Kokura Gion Daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459006}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Life of Matsu the Untamed|qq=|1=Muhōmatsu no Isshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24890846}}'' featuring actors like [[:en:Tsumasaburō Bandō|Bando Tsumasaburō]] (1943 version) and [[:en:Toshiro Mifune|Mifune Toshirō]] (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Timeline == * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (era)]] 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}} from Yasaka in [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao, Kokuraminami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiku District|qq=|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}. * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 7 (1602) – [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] begins constructing [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanga Market|qq=|1=Tanga Market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510878}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtemachi Interchange|qq=|1=Ōtemachi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258134}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Interchange|qq=|1=Katsuyama Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258218}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] * [[:en:Riverwalk Kitakyūshū|Riverwalk Kitakyushu]] * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|qq=|1=Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400235}} == South Hall:
[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|qq=|1=Jōnai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425982}}, [[:en:Kokurakita Ward|Kokurakita Ward]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Taiko|qq=|1=Kokura Gion Daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459006}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Life of Matsu the Untamed|qq=|1=Muhōmatsu no Isshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24890846}}'' featuring actors like [[:en:Tsumasaburō Bandō|Bando Tsumasaburō]] (1943 version) and [[:en:Toshiro Mifune|Mifune Toshirō]] (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Timeline == * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (era)]] 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}} from Yasaka in [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao, Kokuraminami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiku District|qq=|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}. * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 7 (1602) – [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] begins constructing [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanga Market|qq=|1=Tanga Market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510878}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtemachi Interchange|qq=|1=Ōtemachi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258134}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Interchange|qq=|1=Katsuyama Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258218}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] * [[:en:Riverwalk Kitakyūshū|Riverwalk Kitakyushu]] * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|qq=|1=Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400235}} == [[sn:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)]][[ja:八坂神社 (北九州市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11390696]] Wikidata name: Yasaka Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan02s3.jpg|300px]] | name = Yasaka Shrine | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Kokurakita Ward|Kokurakita Ward]], {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|1=Jōnai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425982}} 2-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|07.68|N|130|52|25.39|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yasaka Shrine}} | Deities = North Hall:
{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐能袁命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之菩卑命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之忍穂耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame no Oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q462555}}, {{ill|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|天津日子根命|1=Amatsuhikone|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アマツヒコネ}}, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|活津日子根命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=イクツヒコネ|1=Ikutsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11147583}}, {{ill|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|熊野久須比命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市寸島比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=タキリビメ|1=Takiribime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932870}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
South Hall:
[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|1=Jōnai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425982}}, [[:en:Kokurakita Ward|Kokurakita Ward]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Taiko|1=Kokura Gion Daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459006}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Life of Matsu the Untamed|1=Muhōmatsu no Isshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24890846}}'' featuring actors like [[:en:Tsumasaburō Bandō|Bando Tsumasaburō]] (1943 version) and [[:en:Toshiro Mifune|Mifune Toshirō]] (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Timeline == * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (era)]] 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}} from Yasaka in [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiku District|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}. * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 7 (1602) – [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] begins constructing [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanga Market|1=Tanga Market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510878}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtemachi Interchange|1=Ōtemachi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258134}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Interchange|1=Katsuyama Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258218}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] * [[:en:Riverwalk Kitakyūshū|Riverwalk Kitakyushu]] * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|1=Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400235}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (北九州市)|version=103722451|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)]][[ja:八坂神社 (北九州市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11390696]] Wikidata name: Yasaka Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan02s3.jpg|300px]] | name = Yasaka Shrine | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Kokurakita Ward|Kokurakita Ward]], {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|1=Jōnai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425982}} 2-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|07.68|N|130|52|25.39|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yasaka Shrine}} | Deities = North Hall:
{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐能袁命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之菩卑命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame no Oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q462555}}, {{ill|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|天津日子根命|1=Amatsuhikone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アマツヒコネ}}, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|活津日子根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=イクツヒコネ|1=Ikutsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11147583}}, {{ill|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|熊野久須比命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=タキリビメ|1=Takiribime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932870}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
South Hall:
[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|1=Jōnai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425982}}, [[:en:Kokurakita Ward|Kokurakita Ward]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Taiko|1=Kokura Gion Daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459006}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Life of Matsu the Untamed|1=Muhōmatsu no Isshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24890846}}'' featuring actors like [[:en:Tsumasaburō Bandō|Bando Tsumasaburō]] (1943 version) and [[:en:Toshiro Mifune|Mifune Toshirō]] (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Timeline == * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (era)]] 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}} from Yasaka in [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiku District|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}. * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 7 (1602) – [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] begins constructing [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanga Market|1=Tanga Market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510878}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtemachi Interchange|1=Ōtemachi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258134}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Interchange|1=Katsuyama Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258218}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] * [[:en:Riverwalk Kitakyūshū|Riverwalk Kitakyushu]] * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|1=Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400235}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (北九州市)|version=103722451|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%8C%97%E4%B9%9D%E5%B7%9E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} (Kitakyushu)}} [[ja:八坂神社 (北九州市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11390696}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

South Hall:
[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|1=Jōnai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425982}}, [[:en:Kokurakita Ward|Kokurakita Ward]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Taiko|1=Kokura Gion Daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459006}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Life of Matsu the Untamed|1=Muhōmatsu no Isshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24890846}}'' featuring actors like [[:en:Tsumasaburō Bandō|Bando Tsumasaburō]] (1943 version) and [[:en:Toshiro Mifune|Mifune Toshirō]] (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Timeline == * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (era)]] 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}} from Yasaka in [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiku District|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}. * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 7 (1602) – [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] begins constructing [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanga Market|1=Tanga Market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510878}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtemachi Interchange|1=Ōtemachi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258134}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Interchange|1=Katsuyama Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258218}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] * [[:en:Riverwalk Kitakyūshū|Riverwalk Kitakyushu]] * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|1=Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400235}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (北九州市)|version=103722451|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural South Hall:
[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|1=Jōnai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425982}}, [[:en:Kokurakita Ward|Kokurakita Ward]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Taiko|1=Kokura Gion Daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459006}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Life of Matsu the Untamed|1=Muhōmatsu no Isshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24890846}}'' featuring actors like [[:en:Tsumasaburō Bandō|Bando Tsumasaburō]] (1943 version) and [[:en:Toshiro Mifune|Mifune Toshirō]] (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Timeline == * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (era)]] 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}} from Yasaka in [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiku District|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}. * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 7 (1602) – [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] begins constructing [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanga Market|1=Tanga Market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510878}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtemachi Interchange|1=Ōtemachi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258134}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Interchange|1=Katsuyama Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258218}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] * [[:en:Riverwalk Kitakyūshū|Riverwalk Kitakyushu]] * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|1=Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400235}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (北九州市)|version=103722451|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐能袁命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之菩卑命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame no Oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q462555}}, {{ill|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|天津日子根命|1=Amatsuhikone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アマツヒコネ}}, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|活津日子根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=イクツヒコネ|1=Ikutsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11147583}}, {{ill|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|熊野久須比命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=タキリビメ|1=Takiribime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932870}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
South Hall:
[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|1=Jōnai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425982}}, [[:en:Kokurakita Ward|Kokurakita Ward]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Taiko|1=Kokura Gion Daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459006}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Life of Matsu the Untamed|1=Muhōmatsu no Isshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24890846}}'' featuring actors like [[:en:Tsumasaburō Bandō|Bando Tsumasaburō]] (1943 version) and [[:en:Toshiro Mifune|Mifune Toshirō]] (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Timeline == * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (era)]] 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}} from Yasaka in [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiku District|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}. * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 7 (1602) – [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] begins constructing [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanga Market|1=Tanga Market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510878}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtemachi Interchange|1=Ōtemachi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258134}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Interchange|1=Katsuyama Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258218}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] * [[:en:Riverwalk Kitakyūshū|Riverwalk Kitakyushu]] * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|1=Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400235}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (北九州市)|version=103722451|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%8C%97%E4%B9%9D%E5%B7%9E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)]][[ja:八坂神社 (北九州市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11390696]] Wikidata name: Yasaka Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan02s3.jpg|300px]] | name = Yasaka Shrine | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Kokurakita Ward|Kokurakita Ward]], {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|1=Jōnai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425982}} 2-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|53|07.68|N|130|52|25.39|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yasaka Shrine}} | Deities = North Hall:
{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐能袁命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之菩卑命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame no Oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q462555}}, {{ill|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|天津日子根命|1=Amatsuhikone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アマツヒコネ}}, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|活津日子根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=イクツヒコネ|1=Ikutsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11147583}}, {{ill|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|熊野久須比命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=タキリビメ|1=Takiribime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932870}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
South Hall:
[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|1=Jōnai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425982}}, [[:en:Kokurakita Ward|Kokurakita Ward]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Taiko|1=Kokura Gion Daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459006}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Life of Matsu the Untamed|1=Muhōmatsu no Isshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24890846}}'' featuring actors like [[:en:Tsumasaburō Bandō|Bando Tsumasaburō]] (1943 version) and [[:en:Toshiro Mifune|Mifune Toshirō]] (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Timeline == * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (era)]] 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}} from Yasaka in [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiku District|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}. * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 7 (1602) – [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] begins constructing [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanga Market|1=Tanga Market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510878}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtemachi Interchange|1=Ōtemachi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258134}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Interchange|1=Katsuyama Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258218}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] * [[:en:Riverwalk Kitakyūshū|Riverwalk Kitakyushu]] * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|1=Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400235}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (北九州市)|version=103722451|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%8C%97%E4%B9%9D%E5%B7%9E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐能袁命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之菩卑命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame no Oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q462555}}, {{ill|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|天津日子根命|1=Amatsuhikone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アマツヒコネ}}, {{ill|Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja|活津日子根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=イクツヒコネ|1=Ikutsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11147583}}, {{ill|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|熊野久須比命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=タキリビメ|1=Takiribime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932870}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
South Hall:
[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少比古那命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛名田比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | type = [[Ken-sha]] (as of May 15, [[Meiji]] 6) | Established = By [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617), February | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri style | Festival = Gion Festival ([[Kokura Gion Daiko]]) on the third Saturday and Sunday of July | Rituals = }} [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan03s3.jpg|thumb|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan08s3.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:140721 Yasaka-jinja Kitakyushu Japan09s3.jpg|thumb|East Gate]] '''Kokura Gion Yasaka Shrine''' (こくらぎおんやさかじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Jōnai|ja|城内 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnai|1=Jōnai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425982}}, [[:en:Kokurakita Ward|Kokurakita Ward]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines deities as listed in the template above. Its previous Ranking under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] was as a [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine). == Overview == Historically called “Kokura no Gion-san,” the shrine is known for the {{ill|Kokura Gion Taiko|ja|小倉祇園太鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Taiko|1=Kokura Gion Daiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459006}} festival depicted in the film adaptations of ''{{ill|The Life of Matsu the Untamed|ja|無法松の一生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Life of Matsu the Untamed|1=Muhōmatsu no Isshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24890846}}'' featuring actors like [[:en:Tsumasaburō Bandō|Bando Tsumasaburō]] (1943 version) and [[:en:Toshiro Mifune|Mifune Toshirō]] (1958 version). The festival, first celebrated in [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 4 (1618), has over 390 years of history and now takes place annually on the third Saturday and Sunday of July. The shrine attracts the largest number of visitors in Kitakyushu and the surrounding areas. Initially called {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}}, the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine during the [[Shinbutsu bunri|Shinto-Buddhism Separation Order]] of [[Meiji]] 4 (1868), which resulted in the broader [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] movement. The shrine is currently an independent entity, unaffiliated with the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Timeline == * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (era)]] 12 (870), April – To combat an epidemic, the {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt=Gion-sha|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-sha|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=祇園神社}} from Yasaka in [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]] was enshrined at Nagao (near present-day {{ill|Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja|長尾 (小倉南区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagao, Kokuraminami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), becoming the regional guardian of {{ill|Kiku District|ja|企救郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiku District|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}. * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 7 (1602) – [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] begins constructing [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]]. * [[Genna (era)|Genna]] 3 (1617) – The shrine is moved to its current location in Kokura Castle’s northern grounds as the clan guardian shrine. The southern and northern halls house deities transferred from Nagao and Sambommatsu (present-day {{ill|Tanga Market|ja|旦過市場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanga Market|1=Tanga Market|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510878}}), respectively. * Genna 4 (1618) – Inspired by the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] in Kyoto, Hosokawa introduces the Kokura Gion Festival, initially featuring decorated floats. * [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), May 15 – Designated a Ken-sha. * [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 18 (1943), October 28 – The release of ''The Life of Matsu the Untamed'' popularizes the Kokura Gion Daiko nationally. * Shōwa 33 (1958), April 3 – Kokura Gion Daiko is designated an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] by Fukuoka Prefecture. * Shōwa 62 (1987) – The current festival schedule is established. == Access == * A 4-minute (350 m) walk from [[Nishikokura Station]]. * Approximately 1.5 km from the [[Kitakyushu Urban Expressway]]’s {{ill|Ōtemachi Interchange|ja|大手町出入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtemachi Interchange|1=Ōtemachi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258134}} and 1.1 km from {{ill|Katsuyama Interchange|ja|勝山出入口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Interchange|1=Katsuyama Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2258218}}. == Nearby Attractions == * [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] * [[:en:Riverwalk Kitakyūshū|Riverwalk Kitakyushu]] * [[Kitakyushu City Hall]] * {{ill|Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|ja|勝山公園 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|1=Katsuyama Park (Kitakyushu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400235}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yasaka-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} * [http://www.yasaka-jinja.com/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (北九州市)|version=103722451|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:%E5%85%AB%E5%9D%82%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%8C%97%E4%B9%9D%E5%B7%9E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} (Kitakyushu)}} [[ja:八坂神社 (北九州市)]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]
